_meta.description: 四次危机的月度指标轨迹，按 t=危机起点对齐，方便和当下 2026 比较。每个事件都标注了它对应的总统选举（如果有）和最终政治结果。
_meta.warning: 这些数字是从 EIA / BLS / Gallup 历史档案拼接出来的，部分月份做了线性插值。正式分析前应核对原始来源。

1980_iran_iraq.label: 1980 伊朗革命+两伊战争
1980_iran_iraq.outcome: Carter 惨败 489-49 EC，普选 41% vs Reagan 50.7%
1980_iran_iraq.outcome_en: Carter lost 489-49 EC, popular vote 41% vs Reagan 50.7%
1980_iran_iraq.key_dynamic: 持续 18+ 个月的波斯湾溢价；通胀峰 14.8%；消费者信心从 75 跌到 51 再回升到 64 但已无救

1990_gulf_war.label: 1990-91 海湾战争
1990_gulf_war.outcome: Bush 1992 败给 Clinton；两党票 46.5% vs 53.5%；Fair 模型预测 Bush 赢 55%（最著名失败案例）
1990_gulf_war.outcome_en: Bush 41 lost to Clinton in 1992; two-party 46.5% vs 53.5%; Fair model predicted Bush win at 55% (most famous failure case)
1990_gulf_war.key_dynamic: 短促油价冲击 4 mb/d 离市；Desert Storm 创下 89% 战时支持率峰值；但 ICS 已经先崩到 64，1992 选民拒绝奖励军事胜利

2008_oil_peak.label: 2008 油价峰值（$147）
2008_oil_peak.anchor_event: 次贷危机苗头 + 油价启动
2008_oil_peak.outcome: Obama 53.7% vs McCain 46.3%；Fair 预测 McCain 48%（接近）
2008_oil_peak.outcome_en: Obama 53.7% vs McCain 46.3%; Fair predicted McCain 48% (close)
2008_oil_peak.key_dynamic: 油价 $60 → $147（最大和平时期油价冲击）；ICS 96 → 55；Lehman 9月叠加放大效应；但因为开放席位，DPER 项归零反而拖累共和党

2026_current.label: 2026 霍尔木兹危机（当前进行中）
2026_current.anchor_event: 美以空袭伊朗 + 哈梅内伊被刺
2026_current.key_dynamic: 波斯湾地缘政治 + 通胀温和但消费者信心已崩。属于 Stantcheva 'cumulative price level memory' 模式。

2022_russia_ukraine.label: 2022 俄乌战争油价冲击
2022_russia_ukraine.outcome_midterm: 民主党失 9 席（vs 预期失 30+）；Dobbs 抵消油价冲击
2022_russia_ukraine.outcome_midterm_en: Democrats lost 9 seats (vs expected 30+); Dobbs offset the oil shock
2022_russia_ukraine.outcome_pres: 拜登退选 / Harris 输给 Trump，两党票 48.3% vs 51.7%；'累积价格记忆'拖累 24 个月
2022_russia_ukraine.outcome_pres_en: Biden withdrew / Harris lost to Trump, two-party 48.3% vs 51.7%; 'cumulative price memory' weighed for 24 months
2022_russia_ukraine.outcome_en: Midterms: Dems -9 seats (Dobbs offset oil shock); 2024 Pres: Harris lost 48.3% vs Trump 51.7%
2022_russia_ukraine.key_dynamic: 油价 $90 → $128 短促但叠加既有通胀；ICS 67 → 50；2024 选举里被'累积通胀'惯性拖死

current_2026_anchor.anchor_event: 美以空袭伊朗 + 哈梅内伊被刺

2022_at_t2.outcome_at_t9: Dems -9 House (better than feared); but lost 2024

2026_today.outcome_pending: 进行中：距 2026 中期 8 个月，距 2028 大选 33 个月

comparison_at_t2.key_insight:
2026 当下（t=2）的 Michigan 消费者信心指数为 53，低于过去 50 年所有四次能源-政治危机的同期水平：1980 年 1 月为 67，1990 年 10 月为 64，2008 年 2 月为 70，2022 年 4 月为 65。然而 2026 当下的 CPI 通胀仅为 3.3%，远低于 2022 同期的 8.3% 与 1980 同期的 13.9%。

这种『低通胀 + 极低信心』的组合在历史上是异常的——通常消费者信心与通胀率高度负相关，但当下出现通胀温和、信心却已崩盘。Stantcheva (2024 NBER) 论文将此称为『累积价格水平记忆』效应：选民厌恶的不是当期通胀率，而是过去几年累计的物价水平偏离基线，因此通胀回落到温和水平后，政治压力仍持续存在。

这种经济数据与选民感知严重背离的状态，过去 50 年仅出现于 1992（老布什执政期 GDP 回升但选民感知仍认为是衰退，最终败选）和 2024（拜登执政期通胀回落但选民仍痛恨累积价格上涨，哈里斯败选）。两次共同特征是经济模型预测执政党赢、实际却败。当下 2026 的背离程度比这两次更严重，对共和党 2026 中期 + 2028 大选构成显著政治风险。
comparison_at_t2.key_insight_en:
Michigan Consumer Sentiment at t=2 (currently April 2026) is 53 — lower than the same-period reading in all four major energy-political crises of the past 50 years: January 1980 was 67, October 1990 was 64, February 2008 was 70, April 2022 was 65. Yet 2026 CPI inflation is only 3.3%, far below 2022's 8.3% or 1980's 13.9% at the same offset.

This 'low inflation + extremely low confidence' combination is historically anomalous — voter sentiment is normally highly negatively correlated with inflation, but we now see mild inflation with collapsed confidence. Stantcheva (2024 NBER) terms this the 'cumulative price level memory' effect: voters dislike not the current inflation rate but the cumulative deviation of price levels from baseline over the past few years. Political pressure persists even after inflation moderates.

This severe data-perception divergence has occurred only twice in the past 50 years: 1992 (Bush 41 saw GDP recover but voters still perceived recession — he lost) and 2024 (Biden saw inflation moderate but voters resented cumulative price increases — Harris lost). Common feature: economic models predicted the incumbent party would win; reality went the other way. The 2026 divergence is more severe than either, posing significant political risk to Republicans in the 2026 midterms and 2028 presidential.
